title: Chittaranjan Palace
tagline: Chittaranjan Palace — heritage city of Karnataka

overview.short: Chittaranjan Palace is a lesser-known palace at Mysore that was originally built for a princess of the Mysore royal family.
overview.description: Chittaranjan Palace is a lesser-known palace at Mysore that was originally built for a princess of the Mysore royal family. It presently houses the "Green Hotel". It is a small hotel with 31 rooms and is eco-friendly (solar power, no AC, no TV, etc.). The profits from the hotel are given to charity.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: BM Habitat Mall
topPlaces.description: BM Habitat Mall is a shopping mall in the Indian city of Mysore, Karnataka. It is located behind the BM hospital in Jayalakshmipuram.

topPlaces.name: Jayalakshmipuram
topPlaces.description: Jayalakshmipuram is an urban commercial and residential suburb of Mysore city in Karnataka state, India. Residence of Noted Kannada Poet Javaregowda is located in Jayalakshmipuram.

topPlaces.name: Jayalakshmi Vilas
topPlaces.description: Jayalakshmi Vilas Mansion is a heritage building in Mysore.

topPlaces.name: Folklore Museum (Mysuru)
topPlaces.description: The Folklore Museum at Mysore, India, is a museum which exhibits folk art and crafts from all over the state of Karnataka

topPlaces.name: Gangotri Glades Stadium
topPlaces.description: The Srikantadatta Narasimharaja Wadiyar Ground, commonly known as Gangotri Glades Stadium (Kannada: ಗಂಗೋತ್ರಿ ಗ್ಲೇಡ್ಸ್) and formerly known as the University of Mysore Platinum Jubilee Cricket Stadium, is a cricket stadium located in the Manasagangotri campus of the University of Mysore in the southern Indian city of Mysore, Karnataka, named after Prince Srikantadatta Wadiyar. The Chamundi hills provides an idyllic backdrop, and the iconic Kukkarahalli lake is just a big-hit away.

topPlaces.name: Dattagalli
topPlaces.description: Dattagalli is a residential area on the southwest of the city of Mysore, the second largest city in the state of Karnataka, India.

topPlaces.name: Vontikoppal
topPlaces.description: Vontikoppal or Vani Vilas Mohalla (V. V. Mohalla) is one of the busiest suburbs of Mysore city.

topPlaces.name: Hinkal
topPlaces.description: Hinkal is a Census town situated at the intersection of Hunsur Road aka NH275 and Ring Road aka NH 275K in Mysore city outskirts of Karnataka, India. It is the most populous census town in Mysore district.

items.note: BM Habitat Mall is a shopping mall in the Indian city of Mysore, Karnataka. It is located behind the BM hospital in Jayalakshmipuram.
Jayalakshmipuram is an urban commercial and residential suburb of Mysore city in Karnataka state, India. Residence of Noted Kannada Poet Javaregowda is located in Jayalakshmipuram.
Jayalakshmi Vilas Mansion is a heritage building in Mysore.
The Folklore Museum at Mysore, India, is a museum which exhibits folk art and crafts from all over the state of Karnataka

The Srikantadatta Narasimharaja Wadiyar Ground, commonly known as Gangotri Glades Stadium (Kannada: ಗಂಗೋತ್ರಿ ಗ್ಲೇಡ್ಸ್) and formerly known as the University of Mysore Platinum Jubilee Cricket Stadium, is a cricket stadium located in the Manasagangotri campus of the University of Mysore in the southern Indian city of Mysore, Karnataka, named after Prince Srikantadatta Wadiyar. The Chamundi hills provides an idyllic backdrop, and the iconic Kukkarahalli lake is just a big-hit away.
Dattagalli is a residential area on the southwest of the city of Mysore, the second largest city in the state of Karnataka, India.
Vontikoppal or Vani Vilas Mohalla (V. V. Mohalla) is one of the busiest suburbs of Mysore city.

Hinkal is a Census town situated at the intersection of Hunsur Road aka NH275 and Ring Road aka NH 275K in Mysore city outskirts of Karnataka, India. It is the most populous census town in Mysore district.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chittaranjan Palace?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chittaranjan Palace is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chittaranjan Palace?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mysuru Airport (11 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (3 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chittaranjan Palace?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chittaranjan Palace at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chittaranjan Palace?
faq.a: Stays in Chittaranjan Palace start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chittaranjan Palace?
faq.a: The top attractions are BM Habitat Mall, Jayalakshmipuram, Jayalakshmi Vilas, Folklore Museum (Mysuru), Gangotri Glades Stadium.
